title: Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Haryana

overview.short: Saraswati Conservation Reserve ( Formerly Wildlife Sanctuary (Saraswati WLS), also known as Seonsar Forest, is situated in Kaithal district of Haryana State, India.
overview.description: Saraswati Conservation Reserve ( Formerly Wildlife Sanctuary (Saraswati WLS), also known as Seonsar Forest, is situated in Kaithal district of Haryana State, India. It is spread over an area of 4,452.85 hectares (11,003.2 acres). Kalesar National Park, Morni Hills and Saraswati Conservation Reserve ( formerly Wildlife Sanctuary ) are respectively first, second and third largest forest in Haryana.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (86 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Saraswati Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
